Three-day acceleration pattern (+0.38%→+0.99%→+1.14%) is exceptionally concerning — the market is accelerating, not decelerating, after $44 breach. $45 within $0.50 at current pace — tomorrow possible. ServerMonkey confirms 64GB DDR5 RDIMM ~$1,350 (+11% MoM) and quote windows shortened to 48-72 hours. Broadcom FY2027 AI >$100B confirms demand trajectory has no visible ceiling.

escalatedRisks.risk: 🆕 Broadcom FY2027 AI semiconductor revenue guidance >$100B — confirms AI infrastructure demand trajectory has no visible ceiling
escalatedRisks.status: NEW - June 11, 2026. Broadcom Q2 FY2026 AI revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY). Q3 guidance $16B (>200% YoY). FY2026 $56B. FY2027 >$100B. This directly confirms sustained and accelerating DDR5/HBM/enterprise SSD demand for at least 18 months.
escalatedRisks.source: Broadcom Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call June 3, 2026 / multiple financial media
escalatedRisks.detail: Broadcom's AI semiconductor business grew 143% YoY to $10.8B in Q2 FY2026. Q3 guidance of $16B represents >200% YoY growth. FY2027 projection of >$100B is a staggering number that confirms AI infrastructure buildout at massive scale — each AI server requires substantial DDR5, HBM, and enterprise SSD. The DRAM ETF dropped 20% (6/3-6/5) on a minor Q3 guidance miss vs most aggressive estimates but fully recovered by 6/9 — financial market noise does not change the fundamental demand trajectory.

escalatedRisks.risk: 🆕 USITC 337-TA-1506: MonolithIC 3D patent investigation vs Kioxia + SK Hynix NAND/DRAM — potential future supply disruption
escalatedRisks.status: NEW - June 11, 2026. USITC instituted investigation June 10 based on MonolithIC 3D complaint alleging NAND and DRAM patent infringement by Kioxia and SK Hynix entities. Seeking limited exclusion order and cease & desist. Target date ~August 2027. Second such investigation (337-TA-1492 already in progress).
escalatedRisks.detail: If exclusion order is granted, Kioxia and SK Hynix NAND/DRAM products could be restricted from US market entry. Combined entities represent ~32% of global NAND (Kioxia 14% + SK Hynix 18%) and ~47% of global DRAM (SK Hynix 29% + potential). Near-term: minimal impact (target date ~14 months out). Medium-term: adds structural uncertainty to already-tight NAND/DRAM supply. Monitor for preliminary determination (~April 2027).

escalatedRisks.risk: 🆕🆕 ServerMonkey June 2026 Market Update (published June 10): Inference Pivot + 48-72hr quote windows + 64GB DDR5 RDIMM ~$1,350 (+11% MoM)
escalatedRisks.status: NEW - June 10, 2026. Most significant monthly market intelligence: (1) Inference Pivot - inference now 2/3 of AI compute. (2) Distributor quote windows shortened to 48-72 HOURS — procurement must shift from monthly to daily planning. (3) 64GB DDR5 RDIMM ~$1,350 (+11% MoM). (4) Q2 orders repricing at spot if not shipped before end of Q2. (5) Global IT spending $4.96T, AI spending $2.59T (+47% YoY). (6) CPU lead times 8-22 weeks. (7) Power becoming hard ceiling.
escalatedRisks.detail: This is the most significant monthly market update to date. The 48-72 hour quote window is unprecedented — it means prices can change 2-3 times per WEEK. Q2 orders repricing at spot if not shipped by end of Q2 creates a hard deadline in 3 weeks (June 30). The Inference Pivot (2/3 of AI compute) means more host CPU/memory per GPU, accelerating DDR5 demand.

escalatedRisks.risk: 🆕 SK Hynix 44.2B won (~$28.7M) TC Bonder 4.5 Griffin order to Hanmi Semiconductor — HBM4 capacity acceleration
escalatedRisks.status: NEW - June 8, 2026. Regulatory filing confirms 44.2B won TC Bonder order. ~15 bonder systems. Delivery to Cheongju M15X by Sep 2, 2026. HBM4 ramp accelerating → DDR5 wafer extrusion intensifying.
escalatedRisks.source: Hanmi Semiconductor regulatory filing / TrendForce / Korea Economic Daily June 8-9, 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: SK Hynix placed first public Griffin contract for ~15 TC Bonder 4.5 Griffin systems optimized for HBM4 manufacturing. Combined with SK Hynix's plan to double wafer capacity over 5 years, this confirms DDR5 wafer allocation will continue to be structurally squeezed by HBM expansion.

escalatedRisks.risk: 🆕 Samsung VP Jun Young-hyun — Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Seoul bilateral (June 8): HBM4E+HBM5+LP40 foundry comprehensive cooperation
escalatedRisks.status: NEW - June 8, 2026. Jun called it 'best conversation ever between the two companies.' Topics: HBM4E volume supply, HBM5, Groq LP40 inference chip foundry. Samsung pursuing Nvidia qualification intensifies HBM wafer competition.
escalatedRisks.source: TechTimes / Korea Herald / The Elec June 8-9, 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung DS Vice Chairman Jun met Nvidia CEO Huang at Shilla Hotel Seoul. Discussion covered HBM4E volume supply (Samsung shipped 12-layer HBM4E samples May 29), HBM5 cooperation, and Samsung Foundry manufacturing Nvidia's Groq LP40 inference chip. Three-way competition intensifies HBM wafer allocation and further squeezes DDR5.

escalatedRisks.risk: 🔴🆕🔴 Japan WF6 (tungsten hexafluoride) permanent production halt July 1, 2026 — structural semiconductor supply chain crisis (09:46 second pass discovery)
escalatedRisks.status: CRITICAL NEW - June 10, 2026. Showa Denko Kanto + Central Glass (~25% global WF6 capacity) notified Samsung, SK Hynix, TSMC that inventory depleted by end of June, production ceases permanently July 1. Cause: China high-purity tungsten powder exports to Japan ceased January 2026 (60-70% of WF6 cost, +325% to +600-700% YoY). WF6 is irreplaceable for CVD tungsten deposition in DRAM, 3D NAND, and logic chip manufacturing. New supplier qualification: 12-18 months.
escalatedRisks.detail: This is potentially the most significant supply chain disruption in semiconductor history. WF6 is a critical precursor gas for depositing tungsten metal layers in every advanced chip — DRAM bit lines, NAND word lines, and logic interconnects all depend on WF6. With ~25% of global capacity permanently gone from July 1, and 12-18 months to qualify new suppliers, this creates a hard supply cap on ALL semiconductor manufacturing. Combined with the existing HBM wafer competition, MLC NAND shutdown, and enterprise SSD shortages, this could be the tipping point for catastrophic supply failure. The only mitigating factor: ~75% of global capacity remains with other producers (Linde/Air Products/SK Materials in Korea/Hyosung/Solvay/Merck). But demand will immediately exceed remaining supply.

escalatedRisks.risk: 🔴 Samsung Line 12 (Hwaseong) 2D NAND→HBM4 DRAM conversion confirmed — MLC NAND capacity physically eliminated
escalatedRisks.status: SUSTAINED — June 10, 2026. Final 3 weeks of MLC NAND production. Samsung Line 12 physically converted to HBM4 DRAM metallization. 🔴🆕 WF6 crisis adds second supply shock — NAND manufacturing now faces dual constraint.
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung Line 12 ceased 2D NAND production March 2026, physically converted to HBM4 DRAM metallization. MLC NAND final shipments June 2026 — only ~3 weeks remain. Combined with WF6 crisis, NAND manufacturing faces unprecedented dual supply contraction — both capacity (MLC exit) and materials (WF6 for ALL NAND manufacturing) are simultaneously constrained.
